title: Religious Statistics - America vrs the World

transcript.text: Hello. So this video is me saying that America is the most religious country in the world.
Because we have the most amount of different religions because we're the melting pot of the world.
So that makes us the coolest country in the world.
Anyways, so I've got a chart here and you can see it on my website.
But of all the different religions of the world compared to all the different religions of America,
I'm showing how America is a pretty good representation of the world as a whole.
I would say the best representation of all the countries.
So this poll comes from Medicine Online.
It's the United States for 2001 and the world in 94, so it's only a six-year difference.
Now, the most popular religions in the world, the most popular religion is Christianity.
With 33.6% of the world population, 1.9 billion people.
The second most popular religion would be Islam.
So Islam is a little bit more than half as big as Christianity, with 18.3% of the whole world.
The third most popular are non-religious people who are just like,
Well, I'm not religious, I'm not going to talk about that.
923,000, 16.3% of the world.
The fourth most popular religion in the world is Hinduism.
Lots of people in India.
764,013,5% of the world.
The fifth most popular religion in the world is Buddhism.
With 338 million, 6%.
Sixth most popular is atheism.
Wow, lots of atheists on the planet.
It's not because of America.
Well, actually, yeah.
That's 239,000 million people.
239 million people.
With 4.2%.
Seventh most popular is Chinese folk.
With 150 million.
I don't know what Chinese folk religion is.
Maybe it's what was there before Confucius.
That would be interesting.
Maybe shamanism of China.
And then the eighth most popular is just all the new religions combined.
Probably most of those in America.
128 million with 2.3%.
And then the ninth most is native and tribal religions.
99 million, 1.8%.
And the tenth most is others like Taoism, Zoroastrianism, and smaller religions.
I don't know why.
Like older religions, though.
With 20 million, 0.4%.
Eleventh is Sikh.
With 20 million, 0.4%.
And then Judaism in twelfth place with 13.5 million with 0.2%.
Thirteenth most popular is shamanism with 11 million, 0.2%.
Fourteenth most popular is Confucianism with 6.3 million, 0.1%.
Fifteenth most popular is Baha'I with 5.8 million, 0.1%.
Sixteenth most popular is Jainism with 3.9 million, 0.1%.
Sixteenth most popular is Shinta with 3.3 million, 0.1%.
So, as far as America goes, the most popular in America is Christianity.
So it's the same as in the world with 151 million, 76.5% of American population as of 2001.
Other polls say it was 85%.
So, there is, on this poll it says it's a 5%, give or take, but I guess it's 10%.
The second most popular in America is non-secular.
And so that would be, compared to America, that would be the third most popular.
So it's a good representation.
The third most popular in America is Judaism with 3.1 million, 1.3% of the population.
Now, Judaism is only the 12th most popular in the world, so Judaism is, so you could say America is a Jewish country.
I think the biggest population of Jews in the world, actually, is in New York, followed by Israel, followed by Buenos Aires.
So, the fourth most popular in America is Islam, so that's pretty much representative of the world, because it's the second most popular in the world, with 527,000, I'm sorry, 1.1 million.
I've been calling off the numbers as of 1990, so it's been growing, actually.
Judaism has been falling.
Islam has doubled, more than doubled.
So, I guess people are defecting from Christianity, becoming Islam, because of their heritage.
Because Islam is so similar to Christianity.
Monotheistic religion, same prophets.
Anyways, the fifth most popular religion in America is Buddhism.
And that has more than doubled since 1990 to 2001, with an increase of 170%.
So, it's grown more than any other country other than Hinduism, and Baha'I, and New Age, and Sikhism.
I guess some others have grown more, but...
Anyway, Buddhism is the fifth most popular in the world, also.
So, Buddhism in America is perfectly representative of the strength of Buddhism in the whole world, in general.
So, the sixth most popular in America would be agnostic, which is kind of like non-religious, I guess, would be the third most popular.
So, that's representative of the world, also.

And then, the eighth most popular in America is Hinduism.

And so, as of 2001, it had 766,000.
Ninth most popular is Unitarian Universalist.

The sixth most popular in America is Wiccan, otherwise known as Pagan or Druid, with 307,000.
Um, that's 0.1% of the population.
And then, eleventh is Spiritualist.
I don't really know anything about that, but they have 116,000.
The twelfth most popular in Native American religions, with 103,000.
I heard there's only a million of Native Americans.

So, I guess the other ones are just straight Christian, or something else.

Um, so, there you go.
